# Vision

## **Vision (12 months+)** 

## **Audience for broader vision:**

- All Doordash and subsidiary data users  
- All personas ranging from builders to consumers across levels who interact with data

Single unified personalization engine connecting all foundational product and data ecosystems. Data Portal is the unified front door for all things data and is the canonical end-user experience for all users. To minimize context switching and meet where users are, AI capabilities for Data Portal are built to be leverageable across relevant touchpoints (Slack, Cursor, Claude Code, Google Docs/Sheets, GPT/Glean etc.)

Individual and domain level (pod/subpod) context-aware AI that anticipates user intent to personalize content using trusted and certified data assets. ‘‘Outcomes focused’ experiences to answer questions within seconds to a minute. 

Democratize institutional knowledge that gets trapped at a team/individual level across audiences to improve reusability and avoid redundancy. 

Evolve into a ‘trusted analyst /expert coworker’ for every data user from DS/DE/SWE to S\&O/PM/Executive.

Self-aware and self-tuning.

[Figma](https://www.figma.com/board/Bir35GMGE7wUoolVfuT98O/Data-Portal-2.0--Personalization?node-id=221-638&t=xOdcHsF5PWUTSLJV-4)  
![][image1]

# Brief (03/24)

# Data Portal Product Review (Pre-read)

Authors: [Sai Sudhir Vinjamuri](mailto:saisudhir.vinjamuri@doordash.com) (Product) | [Harsha Reddy](mailto:harsha.reddy@doordash.com)(Eng)  
Type: Product Brief \- Leadership  
Read time: \~X min

## **Background Context**

Today, customers rely on dozens of tools to discover and access the data they need to make decisions, ship features, and grow the business, resulting in a fragmented experience. In Q4 2025, we created a design vision to launch a [unified Data Portal](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OcMUfh4pMUz2aZajctsgATezP43I6SxoWDjyIhkA8es/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.grlq7s2kdfr1) as a single tool to improve the discovery and authoring of data. Building on this vision, given how AI models and agentic capabilities are evolving rapidly, we now have an opportunity to build a more AI-native data portal to evolve faster to meet customer expectations. Our primary goal continues to be:

* **Data Consumers (Non-tech):** Enable operators, business teams and decision-makers to independently answer data questions through intuitive AI-powered conversational analytics experiences  to move faster and build better products (reducing reliance on analytics).  
* **Data Builders (tech):** Empower data builders through a suite of AI-assisted builder tools to be able to move faster and build better, improving productivity.

For this product review, here are the goals / non-goals: 

* **Goals:**  
  * Align on the AI-Native vision to include data builder experiences (SQL Studio, In-house BI, Notebooks, AI Workflows)  
  * Share a status update on on-going Data Portal work, demo features and next steps  
  * Share data persona research  
* **Non-Goals:**   
  * On-going work in Conversational analytics / Research track (AskAI, DataDog). We will cover those topics in a future review with a plan for how those work streams will merge as a follow-up.  
  * Notebooks UX (WIP)  
  * In-House BI (WIP)

**Problems**  
     **User Research**  
     **Why does this matter now?**  
**Opportunity**  
**Vision \+ Proposed Solution**

* **Data Consumers:** Enable operators and business teams to independently answer data questions through intuitive AI-powered interfaces to move faster and build better products.  
* **Data Builders:** Empower data builders through a suite of AI-powered builder tools to be able to move faster and build better, improving productivity.

**Status Update/Demo of Data Portal 2.0**  
**Impact**  
**Key Assumptions**  
**Key Risks**  
**Discussion Topics / Feedback Sought**  
**Appendix**  
    **Roadmap**  
    **FAQs**

# AI Learnings

# Brief \- P13N

# Data Portal Product 

Authors: [Sai Sudhir Vinjamuri](mailto:saisudhir.vinjamuri@doordash.com) (Product)   
Team:  [Jacopo Himberg](mailto:jacopo@wolt.com) (Eng) | [Harsha Reddy](mailto:harsha.reddy@doordash.com) (Eng) | [David Lin](mailto:david.lin@doordash.com) (Product)

—------------------------------------------------------------------  
Questions to answer/Topics to cover:

1. Deliver an understanding of all Personas \+ JTBDs \+ Problems across Doordash  
2. What are their typical workflows? (need to do Deeper discovery)  
   1. Identify a couple of meaty E2E journeys  
3. What problems are you trying to solve with Personalization? Why?   
4. Vision \- 12-24 months  
5. Map out a couple of E2E journeys (with friction today) that can be solved?  
6. DP Prototype (edit this): [https://www.figma.com/make/MztA50vJemJX2Me5M57lFW/Data-Portal-2.0---Top-AI-bar?p=f\&t=D1DWzc5HD9Koiluh-0](https://www.figma.com/make/MztA50vJemJX2Me5M57lFW/Data-Portal-2.0---Top-AI-bar?p=f&t=D1DWzc5HD9Koiluh-0)

—------------------------------------------------------------------

## **Background Context**

There are about 1,500 employees across S\&O, data scientists, and data engineers who are customers of our data platform. These customers use data on a daily basis to help make decisions, ship features, and grow the business. Today, they use dozens of tools to discover and author data which gives them a fragmented experience.  

In Q4 2025, we created a design vision to launch a [unified Data Portal](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OcMUfh4pMUz2aZajctsgATezP43I6SxoWDjyIhkA8es/edit?usp=sharing) as a single tool to improve the discovery and authoring of data. But the breadth of our data ecosystem means that *finding the right thing at the right time by the right user* is one of the biggest points of friction across users. Personalization can transform that experience, and can accelerate decision making across the org. This product doc outlines an approach to build a personalization layer that enables highly tailored user experiences based on user role type, function/domain, technical depth and context.

**Problems and opportunities**  
Data Portal will be the canonical home for Doordash’s data, but its scale and variety create friction for users trying to get work done. [Business Intelligence - Customer Matrix](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/15sSatlejkHTgHjgSY06ojR6qX4abb37z39RCrnAS4WA/edit?usp=sharing)

* **One size fits all experience:** Data Portal MVP treats all users the same, forcing data scientists, S\&O (business analysts) and Executives to hunt for what’s relevant to their role, tenure and context.   
* **Overwhelming choice & low signal to noise:** Users face a large catalog of datasets, metrics, dashboards and pipelines with poor prioritization for their needs, so finding the right asset is time consuming. For example, ‘Phantom’ dashboards & metrics exist where people see views but can’t tell how they’re built.  
* **Context disconnect:** Search and Discovery along with landing page ignore role, tenure, recent work, and organization semantics (teams / metrics / OKRs / authoritative sources), so content/results lack the context that makes them trustworthy and immediately useful.   
* **Collaboration is fragmented:** Teams can’t easily share curated views, annotate assets, or see peer activity and decisions in-context, leading to duplicated analyses, slow handoffs, and weak institutional memory. 

**Opportunity**  
Personalization of Data Portal will transform discovery, trust and productivity by shaping the portal to the user, delighting customers. 

* **Make AI / key word search intentful:** Personalize search results by role, team, activity, and organizational semantics (OKRs, owners, certified sources) whereas AI agentic responses are tailored based on role and tenure.   
* **Role/context aware landing page:** Immediately present the metrics, dashboards, datasets, docs, and pipelines most relevant to the user’s function, team, and goals.   
  * Imagine →   
    * An executive user (decision maker) immediately sees relevant top line metrics and dashboards (pinned metric widgets /most frequently visited / bookmarked) for faster decision making.   
    * A data scientist (analyzer) embedded in the Dasher Acquisition team (Dx) sees derived datasets, queries, experiments and dashboards relevant to their Dx-Acq domain for quick analysis.   
    * A data engineer (builder) will have easy access to authoring tools and monitor health (errors/logs) for relevant pipelines.  
    * A S\&O manager (analyzer & decision maker) sees timely metrics, dashboards and derived datasets for quick analysis and decision making.   
* **Contextual recommendations:** Use recent activity, team membership, org/sub-org semantics and query intent to proactively surface authoritative assets.  
* **Make collaboration native:** Team landing pages, in-context annotations/comments, activity feeds, snapshots and shared links, provide shared workspaces, and permission-aware templates so teams collaborate around authoritative assets and preserve decision context.

—-----------------  
Empower every DoorDash employee (including subsidiaries) to instantly access the trusted foundational knowledge, data assets, insights, and tools curated to their context, enabling faster decisions, deeper understanding, and more impactful outcomes across the business.  
—-

## **Themes**

Key themes identified:

**‘Get Started’ Templates**

1. Metrics \+ Metric widgets \+ AI summaries  
   2. Opportunity sizing  
   3. Experimentation templates (power analysis etc.)

**Foundational Knowledge**

1. Team/Domain level Foundational knowledge  
   1. For example, Across DS, S\&O and Product on Dasher team, there are a set of metrics, dashboards and relationships that are common to everybody in Dasher. At a domain level, providing all roles insight into this foundational knowledge will add a ton of value.   
      1. How many dashers do we have?   
      2. How many are on the road today?   
      3. Acceptance rate?   
      4. Dasher pay?   
2. Sub-team / Domain level knowledge  
   1. For example, there are a few assets more relevant to the sub-team within Dasher domain.  
      1. Dasher Acquisition team track metrics related to acquisition  
         1. Cost to acquire more dashers  
         2. Shifts, utilization hours, retention   
      2. Dasher Pay team track metrics for pay  
         1. Overall pay in regular markets vs. regulated markets (Seattle/NYC etc.)  
         2. Pay over time   
         3. Cohorts in 10 years  
3. On-going projects	

**AI Experiences**

- Today DS run through repos / queries on Cursor to identify parent repos  
- Personalized Search   
  - Rank relevant datasets by query volume and show top users who’ve queried datasets along with the ‘CREATE’ ETL to build trust/transparency.  
- Frequentist  
  - Top datasets  
  - Most queries  
  - Here’s what other colleagues are looking at

**Collaboration** 

- Today, conversations happen on slack (or) email where an executive/director circles a chart in a dashboard (annotate) and shares it (or) the other way around for insights. Users look at the visual, comment and tag people on it to make decisions. These conversations get lost in threads and it’s not easy to refer back to a discussion and reuse it. 

**Notifications**

- Proactive notifications for:  
  - DE/DS: stale pipelines, DQ issues, upstream job errors  
  - Drop in metric within threshold  
  - Weekly summary reports

**Customization**

—-----------------------------------WIP—---------------------------------------------

## **Competitive Research**

# PRD

# PRD: Data Portal Personalization

# 1\. Introduction

There are \~XX employees across Data Science, Data Engineering, Strategy & Ops, Product, and Executive teams who rely on our data platform to analyze business trends, generate hypotheses, size opportunities, make decisions, ship features, and drive business growth.

Today, these users navigate dozens of disconnected tools \- search, dashboards, documentation, and Slack to answer even simple questions. This fragmented experience creates significant context switching and cognitive load, often forcing teams to spend more time understanding how something was built than using it to drive impact.

In Q4 2025, we established a vision to launch a [unified Data Portal](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OcMUfh4pMUz2aZajctsgATezP43I6SxoWDjyIhkA8es/edit?usp=sharing) \- a single, centralized tool to improve how users discover and author data. However, given the breadth and complexity of our data ecosystem, helping the right user find the right information at the right time remains one of the most significant sources of friction. Personalization has the potential to fundamentally  transform this experience and accelerate decision making across the organization. This PRD outlines the personalization requirements for the Data Portal and provides context on the problem we’re solving for users, impact and criteria for success. 

# 2\. Product vision

Reimagine how DoorDash works with data \- transforming our platform into an AI-powered, intent aware partner that delivers trusted, outcome focused insights in seconds. By eliminating friction and context switching, we unlock faster decisions, sharper thinking and step change impact across the business.

# 3\. Target users and JTBD

In Q1 2026, we conducted extensive research of roles across DoorDash and identified 6 personas of data users. Research is currently underway for ‘Analytics Engineer’ roles. For more details, refer to [user research](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/15sSatlejkHTgHjgSY06ojR6qX4abb37z39RCrnAS4WA/edit?gid=1846256403#gid=1846256403) on personas. 

For M0 (4/30), our plan is to solve for at least one use case within Ax, S\&O and executive personas. 

| Persona | Primary JTBD | Current Friction | Personalization Opportunity |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Data Scientist (Experiment Runner \- Cx, Dx, NV, Others) | Run experiments end-to-end and turn results into clear decisions | Fragmented experiment context, hard-to-find lineage, unreliable metrics, repeated Slack pings | Auto-surface experiment history, recommended datasets, lineage \+ trust signals, unified experiment summary view |
| Data Scientist (Adhoc / Deep Dive / Funnel analysis \- BizOps, Mx, Ads & Promos) | Quickly investigate anomalies or answer ambiguous business questions | Hard to discover prior analyses, definitions unclear, slow dataset discovery | Context-aware search, prior related analyses surfaced, metric definition overlays |
| Data Engineer | Build and maintain reliable, reusable data assets | Low visibility into asset usage, unclear ownership, reactive support | Monitor health, Usage-based recommendations, ownership visibility, proactive alerts on downstream impact |
| Strategy & Ops (S\&O) | Size opportunities and drive operational decisions | Dashboard sprawl, inconsistent metrics, repetitive pulls | Personalized metric bundles, domain-based landing pages, AI summaries |
| Product Manager (PM) | Monitor feature performance and make roadmap decisions | Context scattered across dashboards, experiments, and docs | Auto-curated feature health view, experiment rollups, performance alerts |
| Executive (CEO/CPO/Finance/Strategy) | Understand business performance and key drivers in minutes | Context switching across WBRs, dashboards, experiment emails | AI-generated executive brief, anomaly-driven recommendations, cross-functional rollups |

# 4\. Pain points

### **Monitor Trends**

**Problem \#1**: **Inefficiency in Dashboard Creation and Time consuming duplication:** SoT Metrics (especially UMP/Data Cubes) can’t be directly used for dashboards without writing more SQL. Also, dashboard makers, particularly those on the Ax team, still need to write hundreds of lines of SQL to display already defined metrics. This effort is estimated to cost Ax team members \~4,000 hours a quarter re-writing queries.   
**Customer Persona**: Relevant to all personas, but primarily for S\&O, Ax and Executives.  
**Use Case:** Proactively monitor the daily and weekly performance of key business metrics to track the health of a new market/product and respond quickly to any emerging trends.

*S\&O Navigate through Complex BI Tools to self-service data*  
*![][image2]*![][image3]![][image4]![][image5]  
                        BI tools                                     AskDataAI agents                    Writing Queries  
*S\&O requesting an Analytics team to either run a query or create a new filtered dashboard, adding unplanned work*  
![][image6]![][image7]  
               Slack                        JIRA Roadmap

**Problem \#2**: **Inability to self-serve deeper views through custom segmentation of data:** With existing BI Tools, it’s harder to slice and dice a metric to create custom views, often having to raise an adhoc request to analytics teams.   
**Customer Persona**: Primarily for S\&O, PMs and Executives.  
**Use Case:** While monitoring a trend, dive deeper into a key business metric by custom dimensions to develop hypotheses.

### **Proactive insights**

**Problem \#1**: **Leaders must switch across multiple dashboards and tabs (or) wait for the WBR to get timely insights for decision making:** Leaders lack a consolidated, real-time view of key metrics and leading indicators, forcing them to switch across fragmented dashboards and weekly WBRs, resulting in delayed insights.   
**Customer Persona**: Executives and Leadership  
**Use Case:** Leaders track consolidated leading indicators in real time to quickly identify performance gaps and want to stay abreast of anomalies, growth spikes, and trends closer to real time to take immediate corrective actions without waiting for WBR.

*Leaders Navigate through multiple executive dashboards to get a quick pulse on business performance*  
![][image8]![][image9]  
               WBR                                           Executive Dashboards

### **Proactive insights**

**Problem \#1**: **Leaders must switch across multiple dashboards and tabs (or) wait for the WBR to get timely insights for decision making:** Leaders lack a consolidated, real-time view of key metrics and leading indicators, forcing them to switch across fragmented dashboards and weekly WBRs, resulting in delayed insights. 

# 

# 

# 5\. Solution themes

# 6\. Milestones

# 7\. MVP user stories

# 8\. Metrics and KPIs

# 9\. Customer journey examples

![][image10]

# Metric Widgets

# PRD: Metric Widgets

### **![][image11]**

1\. Overview  
‘My Dashboard’ is a personalized metric canvas that allows users to:

* Search and discover trusted metrics  
* Add metrics as widgets  
* Choose chart types and customize  
* Pin/unpin widgets  
* Self-serve deeper views with dimensions, filters and time periods

This canvas provides a single, customizable place to monitor metrics users rely on daily. 

2\. Problem Statement  
**Problem \#1**: **Inefficiency in Dashboard Creation and Time consuming duplication:** SoT Metrics (especially UMP/Data Cubes) can’t be directly used for dashboards without writing more SQL. Also, dashboard makers, particularly those on the Ax team, still need to write hundreds of lines of SQL to display already defined metrics. This effort is estimated to cost Ax team members \~4,000 hours a quarter re-writing queries.   
**Customer Persona**: Relevant to all personas, but primarily for S\&O, Ax and Executives.  
**Use Case:** Proactively monitor the daily and weekly performance of key business metrics to track the health of a new market/product and respond quickly to any emerging trends.  
*S\&O Navigate through Complex BI Tools to self-service data*  
*![][image2]*![][image3]![][image4]![][image5]  
                        BI tools                                     AskDataAI agents                    Writing Queries  
*S\&O requesting an Analytics team to either run a query or create a new filtered dashboard, adding unplanned work*  
![][image6]![][image7]  
               Slack                        JIRA Roadmap

**Problem \#2**: **Inability to self-serve deeper views through custom segmentation of data:** With existing BI Tools, it’s harder to slice and dice a metric to create custom views, often having to raise an adhoc request to analytics teams.   
**Customer Persona**: Primarily for S\&O, PMs and Executives.  
**Use Case:** While monitoring a trend, dive deeper into a key business metric by custom dimensions to develop hypotheses.

3\. Goals & Non-Goals  
**Goals:**

* Allow users to search and add trusted metrics to a personal canvas  
* Show a default set of pre-selected metrics based on persona  
* Enable lightweight customization (chart type, time period, filters)  
* Support chart **↔** table toggle   
* Allow pin/unpin \+ reorder of widgets  
* Enable deep dive via additional dimensions

**Non-Goals (V1):**

* AI driven metric creation  
* Full BI authoring tool  
* Cross-widget/metric calculated metrics & visuals  
* Real-time collaborative dashboards  
* Any other advanced modeling

4\. Target Personas

* S\&O / Operators → Track weekly KPIs  
* PMs → Monitor product health metrics  
* Execs → High-level performance overview

5\. User Jobs To Be Done (JTBD)

* When I rely on a metric frequently, I want to pin it to my dashboard so I don’t have to search for it repeatedly  
* When monitoring trends, I want to quickly switch chart types to match my analysis need  
* When investigating a change, I want to slice by additional dimensions without creating a new metric (or) writing another query  
* When reviewing data in detail, I want to toggle to table view for ‘actuals’  
* When priorities change, I want to easily add/remove widgets

6\. Design Prototype   
[Figma Make Link](https://www.figma.com/make/edc7DQ4BoZr3AK4BUR6ztZ/Data-Portal-2.0---Vision-V1?p=f&t=8eJTmVjPqSnlMwE2-0) → ‘My Dashboard’ Tab

## 7\. Core User Flows

| User Flow | Requirements | Questions |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Flow 1: Add a Metric Widget**![][image12] | **\[P0\]** User can search for a metric using natural language **\[P0\]** Search results should show relevant metrics based on intent along with corresponding metric metadata (definition, owner, domain, etc.) **\[P0\]** If a user finds a relevant metric that matches their intent → The user selects it.  If not, display ‘We couldn’t find a close match for “{query}”. Try different keywords’ **\[P1/P2\]**. Later, we can show ‘Browse recommended metrics below’ search results for this scenario (not a P0) **\[P0\]** Users can go back to AI search to look for other metrics.  **\[P0\]** After metric selection, based on the metric measure, suggest a chart type that best fits the measure (Line Chart for trends over time, Bar chart for comparisons/categorical measures, Area chart for volume/magnitude, KPI Chart etc.) **\[P0\]** User adds the selected metric to the Canvas **\[P1\]** Display axis titles if feasible (consider auto generating using LLMs) **\[P1\] Multiple metric selection within the same widget** Cross-tabs / select  multiple metrics for the same widget and plot them in the same chart for comparisons/correlation analyses. | **MVP Scope:**  Keyword Search (or) AI search UMP Metrics (Cube or non-Cube) What’s the metric coverage (%) going to be? What metric metadata is available to be surfaced in the UI? How does this flow look with Thoughspot Embedding?   |
| **Flow 2: Edit Widget![][image13]** | Once a metric widget is added, users should be able to edit this widget to try other visuals, apply filters, or slice/dice data by available dimensions.  **\[P0\]** View Definition: Users can refer to the ‘View Definition’ modal for metric description, calculation logic, SoT SQL Query, Data Sources, Owner, Last updated. Users can switch to ‘View Source Data’ to view sample records from this modal and vice-versa.   **\[P0\]** View Source Data: Users can visit this modal to view sample records (n=10) → Show the most recent records **\[P0\]** ‘CSV / ‘Excel’ download for sample records → This will be helpful especially since most datasets have 20+ columns and will be easier to browse. **\[P0\]** ‘Select Columns to Display’ for an easier browse experience  **\[P0\]** ‘Search’ bar ( key word search) for users to quickly search for a relevant metric/measure/dimension   **\[P1/P2\]** ‘Open in Mode’ bar (give users ability to open this metric dataset for custom views in Mode. However, while we figure our ThoughtSpot embed strategy, instead of Mode, we should redirect users to this TP embed (instead of sending users to a 3P Tool like Mode) **\[P2\]** ‘Add Column’ bar (give users ability to pull in another column from a different dataset/metric for custom views at the same grain.  **\[P0\]** ‘Chart Type’: After making an initial selection through ‘Create widget’ flow, users should be able to edit/try out other views available for that measure.  **\[P0\]** Slice/Dice: User typically would want to go a level deeper while investigating a trend in a metric (which city/market/region, merchant, category etc.) depending on the metric **\[P0\]** Time Period: Users typically would want to go a level deeper to get the latest 1 week/2week trend or look at historical trends. Depending on metric, users should be able to apply custom time period filters (L28 to begin with, but open to longer windows if available programmatically) **\[P1\]** Filters: Surface any cohort level filters depending on metric (for ex. Dashpass users \- a subset of MAUs, new users etc.)   **\[P1/P2\]** Aggregation Method: This is similar to an excel pivot table feature to group the available data and change how the measure is calculated (from sum to average, median, %le etc.) | **MVP Scope:**  Are we able to pull in dimensions for the Slice/Dice feature for MVP? Does every metric have associated dimensions in UMP→ Cube?  How does this flow look with Thoughtspot Embedding?  |
| **Flow 3: Customize your dashboard ![][image14]** | **\[P0\]** Once metric widgets are pinned to the dashboard, users should be able to enter ‘Customize your dashboard’ mode to pin/unpin dashboards and save those settings. **\[P0/P1\]** Drag & Drop to reorder metric widgets on the UI and save those settings |  |
| **Flow 4: Chart ↔ Table Toggle**  | **\[P0\]** For the selected metric/widget, users want to look at the ‘actual’ numbers behind a trend. Using this toggle, users can switch between chart and table views.  |  |

8\. Functional Requirements  
**8.1 Metric Discovery**

* Search by name, tag, synonym (metric semantic layer)  
* Show definition \+ owner \+ lineage (view definition CTA)  
* Only trusted metrics (SoT) are allowed

**8.2 Widget Configuration**  
Each widget supports: 

* View Definition  
* View Source Data  
* Chart Types  
  * Bar, Line etc.  
* Group by dimension (wherever applicable \- depends on metric)  
* Aggregating method (sum, avg, count etc.)   
  * Depending on metric, some aggregation options may not be available

**8.3 Canvas Behavior**

* Drag/Drop & Reorder  
* Pin/Unpin  
* Save layout state  
* Default metrics \+ layout on first visit  
  * We can an even number (6) metrics by default

**8.4 State Persistence**

* Layout saved per user  
* Widget configurations persist

9\. Dependencies

* GET\_SQL Vantage End point (won’t be ready)  
  * Working with UMP Vantage team closely, sequencing needs to be confirmed/aligned

10.Limited Preview Priorities

# **Roadmap**

![][image15]

# **Data Portal \+ Platform Product Roadmap**

This roadmap lays out the sequence and prioritization of key Data Portal features, explaining the rationale behind each phase and the intended user impact. As we gain greater clarity in the coming weeks on the broader direction for AI agentic capabilities, we will continue refining the plan to align with the long-term [end-state vision](https://www.figma.com/board/Bir35GMGE7wUoolVfuT98O/Data-Portal-2.0--Personalization?node-id=221-638&t=ymx5xgEmOGTVFCmL-4).

—-----------------------------------------------------  
Notes:

- 2 parallel tracks \- bi pROBLEM \+ AI problem  
  - BI Problem  
    - Deprecating mode / NBs  
  - SQL Editor / SQL AUthorship  
    - Harsha to share requirements  
    - Not mode; AI Forward SQL generator  
    - Help with pedregal adoption  
    - E2E Flow  
    - Type \-\> Get sql \-\> right dataset  
  - Metrics Builder  
    - Widget  
  - SQL \+ Metric Editor \-\> Long term BI Solution (Visualization)  
  - Mode, AskData, UMP \- \>All collectively into one  
    - Demo use case (E2E Journey)  
  - Embed  
    - Metric builder can be a replacement of a TP embed

Roadmap Items:

* 2 month plan  
* Feasibility analysis

—-------------------------------------------------------

# **BI Track**

| S.No | Phase | Theme | Key Features | Problem Statement | Success Criteria | Dependencies |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | \<Assessing if product support is needed\> | **BI \- SQL Builder** |  |  |  |  |
| 2 | \<Assessing if product support is needed\> | **BI \- ThoughtSpot to Portal Embed** | **Embedded TP Liveboards inside Data Portal Canvas** via APIs/SDK so users don’t get redirected to the ThoughtSpot UI — Portal remains the single front door. | Dashboard \+ BI Tool proliferation. For more context, visit [Unified Data Portal + BI  Consumption Strategy](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1zzwSMrAndBVgQJhdxfSNNOaE3VG-JpMwyIRJOJXwSII/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.csktwu8z3sz1) |  |  |

# 

# 

—-------------------------------------------------------

# **On-Hold** 

| S.No | Phase | Theme | Key Features | Problem Statement | Success Criteria | Dependencies |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | Dev milestone            Mar 6, 2026 M0 \- LiPrMar 31, 2026 | **Discovery \+ Few Personalization Features** | **UI:**  Key word search AI Chat UI Integration **Backend:** Search, Ask Data Explorer | Due to a fragmented ecosystem of dozens of data tools, users struggle with high-friction discovery and cannot reliably find the right, trusted data asset. | Identify bugs and fix it; Chat integration works as intended;  User Testing and quick iterations Adoption:WAU \> 50% of TAM |  |
| 2 | Dev milestone            Mar 15, 2026 M1- LiPr Apr 30, 2026 | **Personalization (Monitor Trends)** | **UI:** Metric Widgets via “My Dashboard” **Backend:**  Metrics: UMP (Similis) Personalization Relevant default metrics | Users are unable to self-serve/monitor SoT metrics and deeper insights through custom data segmentation, leading to inefficient and time-consuming dashboard creation and duplication. | Reduce time to insight for S\&O, PMs & Execs. Improves Self-service capabilities for non-technical personas | Get\_SQL for UMP (Similis) \+ Semantic layer |
| 3 | Dev milestone            Mar 31, 2026 M2- LiPr Apr 30, 2026 | **Personalization (Discover AI Insights)** | UI: AI Overview by Product Area        \+ Metrics/Insights Scorecard       \+ FavoritesBackend:  Metrics: UMP (Similis) AI: Ask Data Explorer Personalization Relevant default metrics in scorecards CEO Agent for AI Summary(?) | Users must switch across multiple dashboards and tabs (or) wait for the WBR to get timely insights for decision making. Proactive insights mined through agents that can be pushed to users via UI/Slack etc. | Reduce time to insight for Executives. Improve discovery and productivity for S\&O/Ax teams. |  |
| 4 | TBD | **Deprecate existing Ask Data UI**  | \<Need more context from [Harsha Reddy](mailto:harsha.reddy@doordash.com)\> Pull resources in 2 weeks |  |  |  |
| 5 | Dev milestone            Apr 30, 2026 M3- LiPr Jun 30, 2026 | **Derived Dataset Authoring ( POC Only)** | Clickable experience to build derived datasets (does not need to hook into backend) | Dogfood GUI authoring of derived datasets | Customer shares requirements for a satisfactory experience when authoring derived datasets |  |
| 6 | Dev milestone            Apr 30, 2026 M4- LiPr Jun 30, 2026 | **Ask AI Network to Portal Integration** | Unify the front door of all AskDataI to Data Portal | Reduce the \# of entry points data customers need to use to get their job done | Customers have only one entrypoint for asking AI questions or building AI experiences |  |
| 7 | Dev milestone            Apr 30, 2026 M5- LiPr Jun 30, 2026 | **Amplitude short term solution** | Allow users to monitor real time telemetry (user behavior in apps) without Amplitude  | New feature launches, executive escalations, and marketing campaigns all need real time telemetry to properly monitor, but we don’t believe the cost benefit exists for using Amplitude | Customers can continue to monitor real time events while we build a more robust long term solution |  |
| 8 | Dev milestone            Apr 30, 2026 M6- LiPr Jun 30, 2026 | **BI \- ThoughtSpot to Portal Embed** | **Embedded TP Liveboards inside Data Portal Canvas** via APIs/SDK so users don’t get redirected to the ThoughtSpot UI — Portal remains the single front door. | Dashboard \+ BI Tool proliferation. For more context, visit [Unified Data Portal + BI  Consumption Strategy](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1zzwSMrAndBVgQJhdxfSNNOaE3VG-JpMwyIRJOJXwSII/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.csktwu8z3sz1) |  |  |
| 9 | Dev milestone            Jun 30, 2026 M7- LiPr Jul 31, 2026 | **7Rooms marketing segmentation on DD (UPSS)** |  | Takes 523 mins to build a 100K customer segment, causing low adoption and churn from 7Rooms paid marketing tools  | 5 minutes to build a 100K customer segment |  |
| 10 | Dev milestone            May 30, 2026 M8- LiPr Jun 30, 2026 | **BI \- SQL Builder** | \<Need more context from [Harsha Reddy](mailto:harsha.reddy@doordash.com)\> |  |  |  |
| 11 | Dev milestone            Feb 28, 2027 M9- LiPr Mar 31, 2027 | **Metrics Authoring (Vantage) (XP Dependency)** | In Data Portal GUI, add semantic definitions to derived datasets, define metrics, and allow ML, XP, and other use cases to source the same metric definition  | Metrics are re-authored constantly throughout the ecosystem leading to poor compute and accuracy issues | 90% reduction in effort when re-using metrics for XP, Dashboarding, and ML |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
